item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Thanks for your work! The first image couldn't be sourced because I took it and manipulated it myself, and I am releasing it under a CC BY-SA 3.0 license. I may find an alternative to the second one at some point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a mention of an included photo, but there is no photo.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image couldn't be sourced and thus is assumed to be non-compliant, and the second image is from AFP as indicated here, and thus is copyrighted. Thus, the images have been removed.

EDIT: The first image was taken by the author and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough, I did mainly check when it was last updated (3 months ago) and not it's exact post date. Doesn't really fix my problems with it though…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was posted 3 years before Annihilation was a book let along a movie…

Though
That movie likely only exists because of "The Color from Out of Space" which in turn very not subtly influenced half the wiki along with a good chunk of surreal fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How can something look like a squirrel OR a monkey? Those are some pretty different animals there.

Overall this SCP seems to have a lot of minor errors, and in my opinion it seems like it's only around because of the movie "Annihilation"
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1000km² at 26 days is not correct

TL;DR: I think you forgot to square the conversion rate.

First: I love that the border grows at "0.8700" meters per hour, with the extra significant figures. It reads like a real measurement. And rate-of-spooky-forest-spread really does deserve this much precision. It's not like "Victims of scary monster die in 88.7644% of attacks," which I see often.

This is a small detail, but good job.

On to the math. The rate works out to ~0.25mm a second. That's really creeping along. 1000km² after 26 days kinda blew my mind, so I had to work it out to convince myself. To make things simple, I approximated the area as a circle with initial radius 20m.

Calculations:

+ show block
– hide block
(1)
\begin{align} Area = \pi r^2 \end{align}
(2)
\begin{align} Area = \pi\: (0.8700\,t + 20)^2 \end{align}

To determine the area at 26 days, I plugged in $t = (26 \times 24) = 624$ and got:

(3)
\begin{align} Area = \pi\: (0.8700 \times 624 + 20)^2 \end{align}
(4)
\begin{align} Area \approx 995363.0351\mathrm{m}^2 \end{align}

There are a million square meters in a square kilometer (not a thousand). So we have:

(5)
\begin{align} Area \approx 995363.0351\mathrm{m}^2 \times \frac{1 \mathrm{km}^2}{1000000\mathrm{m}^2} \approx 1\,\mathrm{km}^2 \end{align}

I think you made a mistake converting m² to km². Specifically, I think you forgot to square the conversion rate. This is a super easy mistake to make. To reach 1000km² = 1,000,000,000m² would actually take ~28 months.

(6)
\begin{align} 1,000,000,000 = \pi\: (0.8700\,t + 20)^2 \end{align}
(7)
\begin{align} t = \frac{\sqrt{\frac{1,000,000,000}{\pi}} - 20}{0.8700}\, \approx\, 20484.2\: \mathrm{hours}\, \approx\, 28 \: \mathrm{months} \end{align}

In sum: after 26 days, SCP-1009 covers only 1km².
SCP-1009 covers 1000km² only after 28 months.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone with grammar skills should do some work on this one. I really like the concept, and the creepy animals. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm hung up on "hog-tied" as well. Why use a D-Class at all when they could air-drop a speaker/camera package into the middle of the thing and see what happens instead? It feels a little "Foundation is evil for evil's sake" and the use of that word only highlights this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd have to agree a little, it doens't seem to murderdeathkill everything that blinks in its general direction, and even if it did, most large cities probably produce enough ambient noise to keep it from engulfing them completely.

I'm also curious as to how it was contained in the first place, for some reason I envisage it involving Gregorian Chanting and a secluded sect of monks. /cliche

Nevertheless, I like it *upvote*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I quite like this, but why is it Keter? It seems to be pretty easily contained and can't really harm anyone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where did you get the idea for this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
selected as a subject, hogtied, and

I really like the concept, but dislike this word. Good experiment log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's adversely affected by salt water and when the Foundation first encountered it, it had taken over an entire (small) island in the pacific and they grabbed a sample and nuked the island? I dunno, but barring something like that, the growth rate alone makes this one hard to swallow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incredible spread rate, nothing natural to keep it in check. I'd be very curious to see how we found out about this phenomenon and began containment on it. If it requires loud noise to keep back, where on Earth is there a naturally occurring, constant loud noise? Much less a ring of noise to contain it initially? Was it an accident at a Foundation site as a result of crossing two innocuous SCPs? Was it an experiment on a previous SCP that, when just the right tone was found, it began expanding, and only through loud noise was it stopped?

Just some ideas.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given how fast this grows, I would think that finding ways to push it back would be a high priority. Would it be as simple as flamethrowers and napalm, or would you have to nuke it to "disinfect" an area? A particular frequency of truly intense and focused sound causes break-down/disintegration, possibly. Maybe nothing at all works, but I feel that it would have been tested and noted in any case.

Also, does it go underground? Is it converting the soil and bedrock to some alien equivalent, or does the effect limit itself to on or near the surface? If it does spread down as well as out, maybe it isn't nearly as well contained as we think, huh?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, but given the way that it seems to look rather beautiful and pristine, wouldn't it be more along the lines of the Hallowed?

Really, there's only one niggling issue with it, to me: the speed of growth. How was this area previously contained, if it has such an esoteric weakness, and it expands so quickly?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Two questions:

1) Shouldn't there be something in the containment procedure about keeping civilians away and/or dosing them with amnesiacs if they get too close?

2) Does the noise keep away birds and flying insects? Or do they naturally avoid the area because of its alien-ness?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, upon rereading it, it's different enough from an idea I've been working on for too long that I shouldn't worry too much about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm really torn… on the one hand, this is interesting and reasonably well done. On the other hand, between the effect and the picture, I can't shake the idea that we've now contained the Corruption from Terraria…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While it's true that if they just found it, it would likely be giant by the time they got around to it, I can think of a number of scenarios that would make it containable: it was created by someone else who knew how to contain it (or the Foundation was around), the Foundation created it accidentally, etc. If I get a lot of other feedback about how didn't make sense, I may add a note- but for what it's worth, I did consider it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Do loud noises cause the area to retract from the source of the noise? If not then how was this contained fast enough to prevent it from growing out of control in the first place?

I'd appreciate a containment log on this one, this side of it is enough for a downvote for me….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP Designation: SCP-1009

It's almost always "Item #: SCP-1009"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is interesting, damn interesting. I don't know how to feel about this. I'm tempted to like it mostly out of curiosity but it doesn't frighten me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you. This is pretty good. Have they tested if lifeforms from within are biochemically compatible with earth life?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops, done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
HORIZONTAL SCROOOOOL!!! GAAH! IT BURNS!

Fix that, please.
